titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/52

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=338 status=live modified=2017-03-30 14:18:48.936592+00 | translation moa=337 status=live modified=2017-03-30 14:18:52.678495+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis . A . archidiaconus . et vniuersus Clerus Laudoniensis Salutem .

nos interfuisse et consilium et assensum prebuisse ad perpetue pacis et Caritatis obseruantiam compositi
oni facte in presentia uenerabilis . Ricar’ episcopi sancti andree . inter Monasterium de Mailros . et ecclesiam de
Dunbar super Decimationibus grangiarum de edmundestune et hertesheued . secundum formam que in cyro
grapho utriusque partis . uel litteris predicti episcopi nostri continetur . scilicet quod monasterium de Mailros singulis
annis xxx solidos persoluet ecclesie de Dunbar . ita quod ecclesia de dunbar nichil amplius supra hos
xxx solidos a prefato monasterio in perpetuum exigere possit uel debeat .

T . Walter archi[diacon]o [written archiado] de sancto
andree . A . abbate de Neubot’ . Gauf’ de dunf’ . Roberto abbatte de scona . Iohanne abbate de Sancta Cruce . Roger
abbate de driburg . Aiul’ . et Patricio decanis . Rob’ de pert Ald’ persona de lawad’ . Gilis de Wadale .
To all sons of holy mother Church, A[andrew] the archdeacon, and all the clergy of Lothian, send greeting.
Those present as well as those to come should know

that we have taken part in and rendered both advice and assent to the agreement for the observance of perpetual peace and charity made in the presence of the venerable Richard, bishop of St Andrews, between the monastery of Melrose and the church of Dunbar concerning the teinding of the granges at ‘Edmuneston’ and Hartside, according to the form contained in the chirograph of each party, or the letters of our aforesaid bishop, that is, that the monastery of Melrose shall pay 30 shillings each year to the church of Dunbar, so that the church of Dunbar can demand nothing more than the these 30 shillings from the aforementioned monastery.

Witness, W[alter] the archdeacon of St Andrews, A[mfred], abbot of Newbattle, Geoffrey [abbot] of Dunfermline, Robert the abbot of Scone, John the abbot of Holyrood, Roger the abbot of Dryburgh, Aiulf and Patrick the deans, Robert of Perth, Ald[red] the parson of Lauder, Gillies of Wedale.
